results.title: Flash-GMM: A Memory-Efficient Kernel for Scalable Soft Clustering
results.content:
Our contribution of an efficient GMM kernel opens the door to many practical use cases. We demonstrate this by focusing on a prominent application: the IVF index coarse quantizer (Jégou et al., 2011b), commonly used for approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. The quantizer is typically implemented via kk-means clustering (Lloyd, 1982); instead, we propose Flash-GMM as a practical and performant alternative (§4). [...] We present Flash-GMM, a fused Triton kernel for efficient computation of
Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs)
over large-scale data in a single GPU pass.
By eliminating the need to materialize the full responsibility matrix in GPU memory, Flash-GMM achieves a 20×\times speedup over existing implementations and enables training on datasets more than 100×\times larger than previously feasible on one device.
To demonstrate its
impact, we
integrate
Flash-GMM into the IVF coarse quantizer for approximate nearest-neighbor (ANN) search.
We show that soft GMM clustering is now a viable drop-in replacement for kk-means, and that GMM responsibilities can be leveraged to assign border vectors to multiple clusters. [...] Furthermore, this unlocks the potential for increased use of GMMs. Prior work has shown GMMs can act as a drop-in replacement for kk-means with improved partition quality across diverse workloads (Patel and Kushwaha, 2020; Liang et al., 2022); Flash-GMM removes the scalability barrier that has prevented this potential from being realized in practice.

results.title: [Literature Review] Flash-GMM: A Memory-Efficient Kernel for Scalable Soft Clustering
results.content:
The paper introduces Flash-GMM, a novel, memory-efficient fused Triton kernel designed for scalable Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) computation on a single GPU. Traditional GMM implementations face significant memory and bandwidth limitations due to the need to materialize the full responsibility matrix $\mathbf{R} \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times K}$ (where $N$ is the number of data points and $K$ is the number of Gaussian components), leading to $O(NK)$ memory consumption and $O(ND + NK)$ HBM memory accesses per Expectation-Maximization (EM) iteration. Flash-GMM overcomes these limitations by eliminating the need to store the responsibility matrix in GPU HBM memory, achieving an $O(KD)$ memory footprint and $O(ND + KD)$ HBM accesses. This allows it to process datasets over 100 times larger than [...] Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo

# [Literature Review] Flash-GMM: A Memory-Efficient Kernel for Scalable Soft Clustering [...] The core methodology of Flash-GMM is inspired by IO-aware tiling strategies, similar to FlashAttention, adapted for the EM algorithm. The kernel processes the data matrix $\mathbf{X}$ in contiguous tiles $\mathbf{X}\_t$ of $B\_N$ rows. For each tile, it performs the following steps within the GPU's on-chip memory (registers and SRAM) across two nested loops:

Outer Loop (Parallel over Data Tiles): The input data $\mathbf{X}$ is divided into $T = \lceil N/B\_N \rceil$ tiles, each processed by a separate GPU block. A tile $\mathbf{X}\_t \in \mathbb{R}^{B\_N \times D}$ is loaded from HBM to on-chip registers. Per-vector log-normalizers $\log \mathbf{Z} \in \mathbb{R}^{B\_N}$ are initialized to $-\infty$ on-chip.

results.title: Flash-GMM: A Memory-Efficient Kernel for Scalable Soft Clustering
results.content:
We present Flash-GMM, a fused Triton kernel for efficient computation of Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) over large-scale data in a single GPU pass. By eliminating the need to materialize the full responsibility matrix in GPU memory, Flash-GMM achieves a 20times speedup over existing implementations and enables training on datasets more than 100times larger than previously feasible on one device. To demonstrate its impact, we integrate Flash-GMM into the IVF coarse quantizer for approximate nearest-neighbor (ANN) search. We show that soft GMM clustering is now a viable drop-in replacement for k-means, and that GMM responsibilities can be leveraged to assign border vectors to multiple clusters. Our approach reaches fixed recall targets with up to 1.7times fewer distance computations, or [...] Hugging Face's logo

# Flash-GMM: A Memory-Efficient Kernel for Scalable Soft Clustering

ibm-research

## Abstract

Flash-GMM introduces an efficient fused Triton kernel for Gaussian Mixture Models that achieves significant speedup and enables processing much larger datasets on a single GPU. [...] targets with up to 1.7times fewer distance computations, or equivalently, yields +2--12 recall@10 at matched computational cost. We release the kernel as an open-source project.

# FlashGMM

Static Badge

Static Badge

FlashGMM Logo

FlashGMM Logo

FlashGMM is a fast implementation of Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) for image compression in PyTorch.
The codes are based on the CompressAI library.

Our paper is available at arxiv. Please feel free to contact me (octachoron(at)suou.waseda.jp) or post an issue if you have any inquiries regarding to this work.

# Overview

The Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) by Cheng et al. 2020 is a powerful entropy model for learned image compression. However, the original implementation is computationally expensive, making it impractical for real-world applications. [...] `examples`

### Evaluation

To evaluate a trained model on your own dataset, CompressAI provides an
evaluation script:

To evaluate provided pre-trained models:

To plot results from bench/eval\_model simulations (requires matplotlib by default):

To evaluate traditional codecs:

For video, similar tests can be run, CompressAI only includes ssf2020 for now:

## Tests

Run tests with `pytest`:

`pytest`

Slow tests can be skipped with the `-m "not slow"` option.

`-m "not slow"`

## License

CompressAI is licensed under the BSD 3-Clause Clear License

## Contributing

We welcome feedback and contributions. Please open a GitHub issue to report
bugs, request enhancements or if you have any questions.

Before contributing, please read the CONTRIBUTING.md file.

## Authors

## Citation [...] Our implementation accelerates the GMM-based entropy model by leveraging parallel computing capabilities of modern GPUs. We achieve this by (1) installing a new search-based algorithm, (2) numerical approximation of the cumulative distribution function (CDF), and (3) leveraging SIMD parallelism in CPU.


Our impelmentation is fully compatible with the CompressAI's implementation. That is, you can apply FlashGMM to any models trained with CompressAI's GMM entropy model without any modifications.

We currently support the following models:

# Installation

We tested FlashGMM on Python 3.10, Pytorch 2.2.2, CUDA 12.1 and GCC 11.4.0. We recommend using Docker container pytorch/pytorch:2.2.2-cuda12.1-cudnn8-devel for easy setup.

First, clone the repository and install the required packages:

results.content:
## Latest commit

## History

## Repository files navigation

# Flash-GMM

A memory-efficient, IO-aware Triton GPU kernel for the Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) E-step at arbitrary scale (Bloch et al. 2026).

## Overview

Computing GMM responsibilities for large datasets is memory-bound: a naive implementation materializes an N×K responsibility matrix that exhausts GPU memory well before the scales where modern applications operate. Flash-GMM eliminates this matrix entirely.

Inspired by the IO-aware tiling strategy of FlashAttention, Flash-GMM computes the full GMM E-step in a single pass over the data, accumulating only O(KD) sufficient statistics. The N×K responsibility matrix is never written to memory.

## Key Properties [...] ## H100-Tuned Variants

For NVIDIA H100 on the paper benchmark workloads (K=1024, D=96–128), we ship kernels tuned for those specific shapes that run roughly 100× faster than TorchGMM: `flash_gmm_h100.py`, `flash_gmm_diag_h100.py`, `flash_gmm_full_h100.py`.

`flash_gmm_h100.py`
`flash_gmm_diag_h100.py`
`flash_gmm_full_h100.py`

For shapes far from the paper benchmarks, or non-H100 GPUs, use `flash_gmm.py`.

`flash_gmm.py`

## Authors

IBM Research

## Citation

If you use Flash-GMM in your research, please cite:

## License

Apache 2.0 — see LICENSE.

## About

Flash-GMM, a memory-efficient GPU kernel that unlocks GMM soft clustering at billion-scale

### Resources

### License

### Security policy

### Uh oh!

There was an error while loading. Please reload this page. [...] This was validated at N=1B vectors (512 GB of data) on a single A100 80GB, completing in ~28 minutes with 1,548 MB peak GPU memory.

## Performance

Runtime of a single E-step (K=1024, D=128, A100 80GB):

| N | Flash-GMM | vs SciPy (CPU) | vs TorchGMM (GPU) |
 ---  --- |
| 10K | 3 ms | 766× | 32× |
| 50K | 9 ms | 1,260× | 20× |
| 100K | 18 ms | 1,458× | 23× |
| 250K | 46 ms | 1,597× | 19× |
| 500K | 84 ms | 1,571× | 20× |
| 1M | 152 ms | 1,738× | 22× |
| 10M | 1,519 ms | 1,740× | OOM |
| 50M | 35,510 ms | 1,752× | OOM |

TorchGMM runs out of memory beyond N≈1M. Flash-GMM scales to N=10⁸ on the same device.
